content:
You have seen infinite scroll a thousand times. You just never had to name it. Open Instagram and keep flicking — the feed never ends. Scroll through Swiggy looking for dinner and more restaurants keep appearing. Twitter, LinkedIn, Reddit, Pinterest — all the same trick. You reach the bottom, and instead of a page-2 button, the next batch quietly loads underneath you.
That “quietly loads underneath you” part is the whole problem. In this article we will build it from scratch with a wall of memes, break it when it gets naive, and then fix it the way you would actually ship it.
Here is what we are building: a page that loads a handful of memes, and every time you scroll near the bottom, fetches more and appends them. Forever.
Live demo: Open the CodeSandbox → Scroll to the bottom and watch the list keep growing.
Breaking the problem into two questions
Infinite scroll feels like one feature, but it is really two small problems wearing a trench coat:

• How do I know the user has reached the bottom? This is pure browser geometry.

• How do I get the next batch of data…
